phases.name: Comms Tab Prompt Automation
phases.objective: Automate prompt-building inside the comms tab so the user experience collapses to standard chat input/output plus a compact operations display showing prompt construction in real time.

tasks.title: Automate comms-tab prompt construction
tasks.description: Move prompt-building components behind an automated pipeline so the visible interface becomes standard chat input/output with a small operations panel that streams prompt-construction steps as they happen.
acceptance: Prompt-building logic is automated instead of manually assembled through multiple visible components.
The comms tab primary surface is reduced to normal chat input/output.
A compact operations display shows prompt construction/provenance in real time.
The operations display is observable without overwhelming the main conversation flow.
The resulting architecture is ready for Victor-mediated automation under Builder Console governance.

tasks.title: Build backend prompt-construction pipeline
tasks.description: Implement the backend path that assembles prompt inputs, applies governed construction steps, and emits structured prompt-construction events for the comms tab operations display.
acceptance: Prompt inputs are assembled by a backend pipeline instead of manual UI composition.
The pipeline emits structured prompt-construction events or provenance records.
The backend path is ready to feed a compact operations display without coupling to the main chat surface.
The design remains compatible with Victor-mediated governed automation.

tasks.title: Build prompt-construction operations display
tasks.description: Implement the compact operations panel that streams prompt-construction steps, provenance, and system state without overwhelming the main chat input/output surface.
acceptance: The operations display shows prompt-construction steps in real time.
Provenance or system-state details are visible without taking over the primary chat surface.
The display consumes structured backend prompt-construction events rather than reconstructing state in the UI.
The resulting interface stays aligned with Victor-mediated governed automation.

tasks.title: Reduce comms tab to standard chat I/O
tasks.description: Refactor the comms tab so the visible primary interface becomes standard chat input/output, with prompt construction moved behind the governed backend pipeline and exposed only through the compact operations display.
acceptance: The primary comms-tab surface is standard chat input/output.
Prompt-building controls are removed from the main visible surface.
The chat surface consumes the governed backend prompt-construction pipeline instead of assembling prompts directly in the UI.
Prompt-construction visibility is delegated to the compact operations display.
The resulting interface stays aligned with Victor-mediated governed automation.

tasks.title: Implement automation audit logging
tasks.description: Add append-only automation run and action audit records with run IDs, governance outcomes, before/after mutation snapshots, actor labels, and a queryable API for overnight review.
acceptance: Automation runs produce run-started, action, and run-completed audit entries.
Audit entries capture governance results and before/after state for task mutations.
Victor exposes a query path for recent automation audit history.

phases.name: Heartbeat Autonomy Foundation
phases.objective: Extend Victor from supervised phase-by-phase execution into sustainable governed heartbeat operation with explicit separation between project build progress and non-project self-reflection.

tasks.title: Separate non-project reflection from Builder artifacts
tasks.description: Move heartbeat self-reflection into a system-level memory/audit path so Builder Console only carries project-relevant planning notes and build progress.
acceptance: Heartbeat self-reflection can be written without attaching to a Builder phase or cluster.
Builder Console continues to receive only project-relevant planning notes and build-progress state.
Automation activity and build progress remain queryable as separate surfaces.

tasks.title: Teach heartbeat to select the next governed slice after phase closure
tasks.description: Allow heartbeat to recognize completed governed phases and either select an existing pending phase or stop cleanly with an explicit recommendation for the next slice.
acceptance: Heartbeat does not stall or fabricate work when the active governed phase is complete.
Heartbeat can identify the next eligible governed phase or produce a governed recommendation instead of looping on a finished phase.
The transition decision is visible in automation audit records.

tasks.title: Prepare unattended 30m heartbeat operation with safe stop conditions
tasks.description: Harden the 30-minute heartbeat path so Victor can run unattended under bounded action budgets, cooldown rules, and explicit stop/escalation conditions.
acceptance: Unattended 30m heartbeat execution respects focus-window, cooldown, and action-budget policy.
Repeated blocks or missing eligible work stop the unattended loop cleanly instead of churning.
Audit output is sufficient to explain unattended heartbeat behavior after the fact.

phases.name: 30m Heartbeat Dry-Run Soak Test
phases.objective: Run a bounded unattended 30m dry-run heartbeat against real governed work and verify safety signals before unattended execution is allowed.

tasks.title: Run unattended 30m dry-run heartbeat soak
tasks.description: Run the real unattended heartbeat loop at 30m baseline in dry-run mode with explicit tick and runtime limits.
acceptance: Heartbeat starts cleanly, remains bounded, and emits audit-backed tick evidence without writes.

tasks.title: Review dry-run soak audit signals
tasks.description: Inspect run state, selected tasks, stop conditions, and blocked reasons from the soak output.
acceptance: The soak produces enough evidence to judge whether unattended execution is safe to promote.

tasks.title: Define unattended execute-mode promotion gate
tasks.description: Turn soak evidence into explicit promotion criteria for unattended write-capable heartbeat operation.
acceptance: Promotion criteria are explicit, auditable, and stricter than the dry-run soak baseline.

phases.name: Forecast and Dependency Operations
phases.objective: Turn the new Victor and Builder planning artifacts into Builder-managed operational work so unattended execution has a governed next queue.

tasks.title: Forecast remaining Builder delivery work
tasks.description: Translate the current Builder forecast into Builder-managed operational tasks and dependency-aware planning state.
acceptance: Builder has an explicit next-step forecast instead of relying on narrative summaries.

tasks.title: Project Victor autonomy dependency impact into Builder surfaces
tasks.description: Expose how Victor promotion state affects Builder delivery readiness without collapsing the Victor and Builder views together.
acceptance: Builder surfaces reflect Victor dependency impact only where it materially affects delivery.

tasks.title: Queue next governed slices across Victor and Builder
tasks.description: Keep the next governed work items explicit across both project tracks so unattended execution advances known priorities instead of fabricating work.
acceptance: The next governed slices for Victor and Builder are Builder-visible and ordered.
